stem: A quality control study surveys 25 manufactured units and observes 1 positive outcomes out of 25 (4.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (12 observations, rate = 8.3%) and Group B (13 observations, rate = 7.7%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 33% and 53% of the population. Estimate the true product defect rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.04. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.0533 (range [0.0478, 0.0588]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.0071, 0.1954]. Point estimate: 0.0533.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.43: 0.43×0.022 + 0.57×0.0769 = 0.0533.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=25, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
